## [00:00 - 03:00]

Hey, this is Deepash and in this video, I'm gonna take you through the 3N ads formula. This is the formula I use when I'm coming up with the creative, the ads for Facebook and trying to kind of capture that attention and that click from Facebook ads as well or the engagement for a video. And so this 3N ads formula is all about how to win the news feed, not from a technical point of view, but from a kind of visual and persuasion point of view as well. What does the N stand for? So the N is Nudge, the 3Nudge ads formula. And Nudge is moving someone from one point to another and in this instance, there are three key nudges that I want to introduce as a concept for you to consider how you actually construct your ads. So from the visual and the copy, et cetera, and how you actually construct that into your delivery within the news feed as well. And so I wanted to kind of pull a description from Wikipedia, which I feel covers the kind of concept behind the Nudge and also how I use it within ad creation as well. And one of the key things it talks about here is influencing the choice that someone has, but in a way that does not force them to do so. So in this descriptor, it says to count as a mere Nudge, the intervention must be easy and true to avoid. So the Nudge in itself has to be fairly fluent. It has to be without kind of friction for the person that you're trying to influence. Nudges are not mandates. So you don't, you know, if you say, this is what you absolutely have to do, that's not necessarily a Nudge. Putting fruit at eye level counts as a Nudge. Banning junk food does not. So some kind of forceful action is not a Nudge. And it continues to go in this form, drawing on behavioral economics, the Nudge is more generally applied to influence behavior. And that's what it's all about. Influence via non-coercive behavior. That's a flash way of saying, get your way without force. And we do this every single day. You know, even if you just say please, that's a Nudge. You're asking for something in return for that please. And it's a soft way of getting your way, essentially. One example I wanted to give around Nudge and kind of influencing via non-coercive behavior is this kind of mini story about urinals. With the urinals, you know, men go all over the place sometimes and it gets messy, let's just say that. What these guys actually introduced was to put a sticker, in this case, a sticker of a fly, in place to help men guide their float. And actually this made a huge difference to cleaning up the urinals because it gave people a narrow to focus on and for whatever psychological reason, it just meant that people were less likely to cause a mess, let's say.

## [03:00 - 04:38]

And this is the kind of kind of the kind of way that a Nudge is created. Rather than having a saying, saying do not do this or putting parameters up, they're using soft influence. And this is such an important part of your creative, whether it's the news feed or in the story or wherever it is, it's all about being subtle in what you want the user to do. So why 3N? There's three parts of the Nudge. Number one is stop that scroll. So Facebook call it thumb stopping content. And you know, it's all about, if you look how busy the news feed is, how can you get someone to stop scrolling? That's the first thing that you have to do. Whether you're on a laptop or a mobile, getting them to stop the scroll is the most important thing. You can forget everything else, even winning the auction, having great copy, the great call to action landing page, even a great product. If you cannot get them to stop the scroll, then the first part of your advertising journey has been a failure. The second part is then around engagement. So okay, you've got them to stop the scroll. How do you keep them there? The first few seconds, probably even milliseconds are so essential here to get your proposition across, get them engaged, get them interested. And then start taking them down that journey. And then the third part is, okay, you've got them engaged. You now need to convert them. And so this section is all about covering those three bases and how you can take someone from stopping their scroll, engaging in your ad and then converting as well.
